operator_inputs_required: named monitoring owner and backup owner
monitoring cadence and SLA by deployment context
incident, customer, and authority notification workflow
retention period and archive location for monitoring artifacts
corrective-action and rollback authority for repeated drift or blocking findings

monitoring_objectives: Detect regressions, approval paths, and blocking findings after release or during release readiness review.
Track recurring drift, risk concentration, and signal accumulation across monitored runs.
Preserve a portable evidence path from current release data to recurring governance review.
Escalate material findings to release, security, compliance, or legal owners before relying on the system in scope.

data_sources.description: Current report bundle used as the baseline evidence source for release or monitoring review.
Trend and watchlist evidence used to compare the current run against prior monitored runs.
Oversight queue and release review used to route approval, blocking, and reviewer follow-up decisions.
Technical scaffolds that keep operator instructions and open risks aligned with monitoring review.

review_cadence.trigger: Release candidate or meaningful model/prompt/tool/workflow change
review_cadence.rationale: Material changes should refresh the monitoring baseline before the system is promoted.

review_cadence.recommended_cadence: Weekly or per formal governance checkpoint
review_cadence.rationale: Regular review keeps drift, approval pressure, and unresolved gaps visible over time.

review_cadence.trigger: Drift, blocking findings, or serious incident signals
review_cadence.rationale: Material post-market signals require a documented response instead of waiting for the next routine review.

review_cadence.trigger: New deployment context, market, or regulatory mapping change
review_cadence.recommended_cadence: Before the expanded deployment goes live
review_cadence.rationale: Monitoring scope and thresholds can change when the operating context changes.

escalation_rules.condition: Execution quality is degraded in the current evidence bundle.
One or more cases recommend blocking release or operation.
Approval-required cases remain open for the current run.
Current monitoring evidence detected drift versus the prior run window.
Monitoring history currently includes the latest run; keep this continuity on future runs.

corrective_action_loop: Record the triggering finding and the affected artifact in the review package.
Assign an owner, disposition, and due date for mitigation or acceptance.
Apply remediation, rerun the relevant cases or release workflow, and replace stale evidence.
Escalate to legal or compliance review when the finding changes deployment assumptions, market scope, or residual-risk acceptance.

residual_gaps: Recurring monitoring cadence, escalation workflow, and regulator-facing reporting remain operator responsibilities.
Operator-authored instructions for use are still required before deployer handoff.
This export summarizes technical evidence; it does not replace deployer-facing operating instructions.
Operator-authored intended-use, deployer-facing instructions, and operating constraints remain required.
Human oversight procedures for deployers still require operator-authored narrative.
The evaluator emits evidence inputs, not the full Article 11 technical file.
The generated dossier still requires operator-authored intended-use, system-boundary, and deployment-context inputs.
